mode.simple_desc: Guided arcade — pick a game, train it, and play. Advanced controls are hidden.
mode.advanced_desc: Full control — every hyperparameter, algorithm, seed, and the Data Lab.
mode.simple_f1: Pick a game and train it
mode.simple_f3: Play it yourself and compare with the AI
mode.advanced_f3: The Data Lab — full experiment analysis
mode.chooser_sub: How would you like to start? You can switch anytime from the top bar.
mode.chooser_hint: Not sure? Start Simple — you can switch to Advanced whenever you like.
mode.eta_slow: A long run — best left going in the background.
mode.eta_patience: This game learns slowly, so you won't see much for a while — that's normal, not a freeze.
mode.chart_status_idle: Press Quick-start to teach the AI this game.
mode.chart_status_strong: Playing well now — it beats a beginner.

tour.title: Guided tour
tour.step_welcome_body: This is a hands-on lab for reinforcement learning: you pick a game, an AI learns to play it from scratch, you watch it improve live, then play against it. This quick tour points out each part of the screen — about a minute. Use Next, or Skip anytime.
tour.step_env_body: The environment picker. Click it to browse 100+ games grouped by category — Classic Control, Atari, Box2D, MuJoCo robots, board games and more; the “Total” badge counts them. Whatever you pick drives the whole screen: the visualization, the recommended settings and the skill scale all adapt to it.
tour.step_algo_body: The algorithm the AI learns with. In Simple mode this is a read-only badge showing the best method we picked for this game (marked ★). In Advanced it's a dropdown over all 9 methods — PPO, neuroevolution, Q-learning, SAC, DQN and more — with the recommended one starred.
tour.step_params_body: Every knob that shapes the run: the hyperparameters (learning rate, discount, network size…), the random seed (same seed → identical run, for reproducibility) and Total Steps (how long it trains). Each ⓘ opens a plain-language explanation, and the green ★ tick marks the recommended value.
tour.step_length_body: Pick Short, Normal (★) or Long. Longer usually means a smarter AI but a longer wait. The coloured note below estimates roughly how long this game will take — and warns you when a game learns slowly, so a quiet start doesn't look like a freeze.
tour.step_run_body: This starts the run. In Simple it's a one-press Quick-start with the recommended settings. In Advanced you can also run a seed sweep — “Run N×” trains the same setup across several random seeds in a row, so you can compare them later in the Data Lab. Once running, this becomes Pause / Stop.
tour.step_preview_body: The live view of the AI actually playing. Early on the moves are random and clumsy; as training goes on they get purposeful. A small badge flashes on each new attempt (episode), and the header lets you turn the view off or speed it up to train faster.
tour.step_chart_body: How the AI is doing as it trains. In Simple mode this is a single plain-language status line. In Advanced it's the full reward chart — score up the vertical axis, training progress along the horizontal — with tabs (Reward / Loss / Fitness), smoothing, a window control and a goal line marking a “solved” score.
tour.step_skill_body: A single gauge rating how good the AI has become — from beginner (red) through the “solved” threshold to superhuman (green). It fills as training improves, and later mirrors your own score when you play, so you can compare yourself to the AI on the same scale.
tour.step_play_body: Take the controls: “Play” starts a keyboard session for you; “AI plays” watches a trained model instead. Choose which saved model and a speed. Your result is scored on the same skill scale as the AI and lands on the leaderboard below.
tour.step_records_body: The bottom row. On the left, High Scores — a hall of fame with the best human runs and the best AI runs side by side. Beside it, live training stats that change with the method: the top genomes for neuroevolution, or the value-table heatmap for Q-learning.
tour.step_datalab_body: Every finished run is saved here for deep analysis — overlay curves from different runs, collapse repeated seeds into a mean ± confidence band, rank them and export the data. It has its own guided tour once you're inside.
tour.step_mode_body: Switch the whole interface here, anytime. Simple is a guided arcade — the recommended method, hidden knobs, one-press training. Advanced unlocks every hyperparameter, all 9 algorithms, seed sweeps and the Data Lab.
tour.step_langtheme_body: Toggle the language (Czech / English) and the light / dark theme here. The ⓘ button beside them opens “About”; the compass reopens this tour whenever you like.
tour.step_finish_body: That's the whole screen. A good first run: pick a game, press Quick-start and watch the skill meter climb — then play it yourself. Reopen this tour anytime from the compass button in the top bar.
tour.step_dl_welcome_body: This is where you analyse and compare finished training runs — like a mini research console. This short tour walks through each panel; Skip anytime.
tour.step_dl_sources_body: Every finished run is listed here, grouped by game and algorithm. Tick up to 8 to overlay them; each gets its own colour. You can also rename, tag, note or delete runs, and group repeated seeds into one named experiment.
tour.step_dl_pivot_body: Two viewpoints: “Per game” plots the raw reward (best for comparing methods on one game), while “Per algorithm” plots a normalized skill-% so you can compare across different games on one scale.
tour.step_dl_axis_body: Plot progress against environment steps (how much experience the AI saw) or wall-clock time (how long it actually took). Steps compare sample-efficiency; time compares real-world speed.
tour.step_dl_controls_body: Smooth the curves (EMA), switch the vertical axis to a log scale, and — when you've selected several seeds of the same setup — collapse them into one mean line with a confidence band. Excluded seeds drop out of every panel.
tour.step_dl_chart_body: Your selected runs, overlaid. Hover for exact values, click a legend entry to hide a line, and read the goal line as the “solved” score. A warning appears if you overlay raw rewards from different games (different scales).
tour.step_dl_table_body: One row per selected run with its key numbers — final and best score, area under the curve, sample-efficiency — sortable by any column, so you can rank runs at a glance.
tour.step_dl_rliable_body: A statistically honest summary of the whole selection: mean, median, interquartile mean and optimality gap, each with a bootstrap confidence interval — the modern way to report RL results without cherry-picking a lucky seed.
tour.step_dl_export_body: Take the data with you: the selected runs and stats as CSV, Excel, a LaTeX table, the chart as an image, or a full reproducibility bundle (config + seeds) so a run can be repeated exactly.
tour.step_dl_finish_body: Select a few runs on the left to bring every panel to life. The greyed “RL Lab” tab, top-left, takes you back to the dashboard. Reopen this tour anytime from the compass button.

about.title: About RL Lab
about.tagline: Build, train, watch, and test reinforcement-learning agents across many games — all in one dashboard.
about.app_body: Pick an environment, tune parameters with beginner-friendly info popups, and train with PPO, neuroevolution, or Q-learning. Watch agents learn live, compare runs, and play against the trained AI with a skill meter. Bilingual (CZ/EN), dark & light.
about.author_body: Made by Martin Svoboda — an independent, open-source project, free for learners. Built as a hands-on deep dive into AI and reinforcement learning.
about.support_note: Free and open under AGPL-3.0. If RL Lab + Data Lab helps you, you can support its upkeep:

topbar.best_alltime: All-time best for {{env}} — persists across restarts

sidebar.title: Parameters
sidebar.algo_count: Total learning algorithms available in the app
sidebar.algo_use_recommended: Switch to the recommended algorithm for this game
sidebar.locked_while_running: Locked while training — press Stop to change
sidebar.run_sweep_hint: Train this exact config across N seeds, queued back-to-back — produces the multi-seed data for aggregate analysis. Uses the seed above as the first seed.
sidebar.train_needs_gpu: Training this game needs a GPU (available on the desktop). You can still play it by hand now — use the Play button below.
sidebar.train_not_implemented: Training for this game isn't built yet — pixel-based games need the GPU (CnnPolicy) trainer that's coming in a later version. You can still play it by hand now — use the Play button below.
sidebar.train_not_implemented_ma: Training for this world is coming in a later step — each species needs its own brain. For now you can just watch the untrained world move (it isn't hand-playable — a swarm has no single driver).
sidebar.train_not_implemented_board: Self-play training for board games is coming in a later step. For now, play against the built-in AI with the Play button below — pick a side and a difficulty.
sidebar.atari_needs_ale: Atari environments need the optional ale-py package. Install: pip install ale-py

stats.no_data: Start training to see live stats

envpreview.title: Environment

envpreview.visual_off_hint: Visual off — training runs at full speed

play.no_checkpoints: Save a checkpoint to watch the AI
play.playing_hint: Keyboard active — see How to play

board.pick_side: Pick a side and an opponent, then press Play
board.training: Training — watching the AI learn by self-play
board.result_draw_watch: Draw — perfect play on both sides
board.cell_select: Select your piece at row {{row}}, column {{col}}
board.cell_selected: Selected piece at row {{row}}, column {{col}} — click again to deselect
board.select_piece: Your move — click one of your pieces
board.select_dest: Now click a highlighted square to move there
board.must_pass: No legal move — you must pass

watch.title: About {{env}}
watch.pick_save: Pick a saved model to watch
watch.train_first: Train and save a model to watch it play

playscore.title: High Scores
playscore.board_deferred: Board games are scored win / draw / loss, not by points — leaderboards arrive with self-play training in a later step.

chart.title: Reward Chart
chart.placeholder: Start training to see the live chart

species.predator_desc: Slower hunters that share a reward for every touch of the prey — they learn to chase, corner and ambush.
species.prey_desc: A faster runner, penalised for being caught — it learns to dodge, flee and use the space to escape.
species.obstacles_desc: Fixed grey blocks scattered in the arena that both species must steer around.

leaderboard.title: Top-5 Children
leaderboard.evo_hint: Run Neuroevolution to populate the leaderboard
leaderboard.best_row: Current best — shown in the live preview

evolution.title: Evolution Stats
evolution.evo_hint: Run Neuroevolution to see live stats

qtable.title: Q-table
qtable.hint: Run Q-learning to watch the table fill in

saveload.title: Save / Load
saveload.nothing_to_save: Start a run first — nothing to save yet
saveload.moved_hint: Save / load is in the sidebar, under the Run button

runs.title: Run history
runs.empty: No finished runs yet — train a run to compare it later
runs.max_hint: Overlay up to {{n}} past runs on the chart
runs.no_overlay: Selected runs have no data for this tab

analysis.title: Data Lab
analysis.subtitle: Experiment analysis
analysis.open: Open Data Lab — compare runs, aggregate seeds, export
analysis.manage_exclude_hint: Hidden from the picker by default — kept, not deleted.
analysis.manage_failed: Couldn't save the change — try again.
analysis.no_runs: No finished runs yet — train a run to analyze it here
analysis.empty_hint: Pick runs on the left to overlay their learning curves. Select several seeds of one game + algorithm to collapse them into a mean ± CI band.
analysis.mixed_games_hint: Per-game mode shows raw reward — not comparable across different games. Switch to Per-algorithm for a normalized (skill-%) comparison.
analysis.chart_aria: Comparison chart of the selected runs
analysis.rliable_pick: Pick runs on the left to see their aggregate (IQM, CIs, performance profile).
analysis.rliable_need_more: No comparable score matrix yet — the aggregate needs seeds shared across the selected games. Select several seeds of an algorithm (ideally on the same game) to get honest confidence intervals.
analysis.profile_axes: fraction of runs (y) scoring above threshold τ (x)
analysis.profile_aria: Performance profile: fraction of run scores above each threshold, one line per algorithm
analysis.poi_no_shared: The two algorithms share no task — no probability of improvement to compute.
analysis.poi_need_two: Add a second algorithm on the same game to compare them.
analysis.rliable_norm: Scores = each run's final skill % ÷ 100 (per-task normalized to [0, 1]); CIs are 95% stratified bootstrap. Wide bands on few seeds are honest, not a bug.
analysis.export_aria_pivot: Download {{fmt}} of the selected runs, {{pivot}} pivot
analysis.export_note: Downloads cover the current selection; CSV, Excel + figure follow the compare mode (per-game raw / per-algorithm normalized).
analysis.export_empty: Pick runs on the left to export them.
